latest.title: Update on Proposed Acquisition
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 1292L
Atlas Metals Group PLC
06 July 2026
6 July 2026
Atlas Metals Group plc
("
Atlas Metals
" or the "
Company
")
Update on Proposed Acquisition
Atlas Metals (LSE: AMG), the natural resources and energy company
,
is pleased to provide an update in relation to the Company's proposed acquisition of Universal Pozzolanic Silica Alumina Ltd ("
UPSA
") (the "
Proposed Acquisition
").
The process to complete the Proposed Acquisition continues to progress and the directors of Atlas Metals and UPSA are now targeting completion by the end of Q3/beginning of Q4 2026, subject to,
inter
alia, the approval of the prospectus by the FCA and approval by Atlas Metals shareholders.
SLR Consulting Australia Pty Ltd ("
SLR
"), which is engaged by the Company, is continuing to work on an updated Competent Person's Report ("
CPR
") using data and results of testing of samples obtained from the drilling campaign undertaken earlier this year, with the target of upgrading the resources from the inferred to measured and indicated. The Company expects to publish an updated CPR in the coming weeks and ahead of completion of the Proposed Acquisition.
Atlas Metals is pleased to announce that it intends to appoint Robert Ober as a strategic advisor to the Board on completion of the Proposed Acquisition. Robert Ober is a highly respected figure in the United States concrete and construction materials industry and currently serves as Chairman of the UPSA Advisory Board. Over a career spanning several decades, Mr. Ober has founded and developed multiple high-performance concrete and construction materials businesses, building extensive expertise in concrete production, aggregates supply chains, batching technologies, and large-scale infrastructure supply. Robert is widely recognised for his deep industry relationships across the United States construction sector and is regarded as a leading authority on the operational and commercial dynamics of high-performance concrete markets.
In addition to the Proposed Acquisition, Atlas Metals and UPSA continue to engage with potential customers and off-takers of the UPSA resource as well as advancing regulatory approvals in Australia.

Total Voting Rights
Atlas Metals (LSE: AMG), the natural resources and energy company
,
in conformity with the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rule 5.6.1R, announces that, as at 1 July 2026, its issued share capital comprised 43,889,001 ordinary shares of nominal value £0.01 each ("
Ordinary Shares
"). Each Ordinary Share carries the right to one vote in relation to all circumstances at general meetings of the Company. The Company does not hold any shares in treasury and therefore the total number of voting rights attributable to the Ordinary Shares is 43,889,001.
The above figure of 43,889,001 may be used by shareholders of the Company as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.

25 June 2026

("

Issue of New Ordinary Shares & Total Voting Rights
Atlas Metals (LSE: AMG), the natural resources and energy company, announces will issue further ordinary shares of nominal value £0.01 each ("
Ordinary Shares
") pursuant to the Company's
"at-the-market" facility with Axis Capital Markets Limited ("
Axis
"; the
"
At-the-Market Facility
"
)
.
At-the-Market Facility issue
1,339,180 new Ordinary Shares ("
ATM Shares
") will be issued at an average price of 7.3 pence per ATM Share, in connection with the At-the-Market Facility. As announced by the Company on 5 March 2026 all proceeds from the
At-the-Market Facility will go towards repayment of the funding facility currently in place with YA II PN Ltd, an institutional investor managed by Yorkville Advisors Global, LP.
Admission and total voting rights
Application has been made to London Stock Exchange plc ("
London Stock Exchange
") for the admission of
1,339,180
ATM Shares to be admitted to trading on the main market for listed securities of London Stock Exchange ("
Admission
").
Admission is expected to occur on or around 26 June 2026.
When issued, the ATM Shares will be fully paid and will rank
pari passu
in all respects with each other and with the existing Ordinary Shares of the Company.
Following Admission, the Company's issued ordinary share capital will comprise 43,889,001
Ordinary Shares, each carrying one vote. The Company does not hold any Ordinary Shares in treasury. The total number of voting rights in the Company following Admission will therefore be 43,889,001. With effect from Admission, this figure may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they determine whether they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.

latest.title: Issue of New Ordinary Shares & Total Voting Rights
latest.content:
Today 16:01
RNS Number : 7604F
Atlas Metals Group PLC
26 May 2026
26 May 2026
Atlas Metals Group plc
("
Atlas Metals
" or the "
Company
")
Issue of New Ordinary Shares & Total Voting Rights
Atlas Metals (LSE: AMG), the natural resources and energy company, announces that it has received a conversion notice from YA II PN Ltd, an institutional investor managed by Yorkville Advisors Global, LP ("
Lender
" or "
Yorkville
") under the convertible loan agreement ("
Funding Facility
") previously announced by the Company on 5 March 2026. Atlas further announces that it will additionally issue further ordinary shares of nominal value £0.01 each ("
Ordinary Shares
") pursuant to the Company's
"at-the-market" facility with Axis Capital Markets Limited ("
Axis
"; the
"
At-the-Market Facility
"
)
.
Funding Facility conversion
The Company has received a conversion notice from the Lender pursuant to the Funding Facility to convert £86,780.82 of the outstanding principal and accrued interest under the second loan of up to £2.0 million, as announced on 5 March 2026, into new Ordinary Shares at a price of 7.80 pence per Ordinary Share. Accordingly, the Lender will be issued with 1,112,574 new Ordinary Shares ("
Conversion Shares
").
At-the-Market Facility issue
In addition to the issue of the Conversion Shares, the Company further announces that 2,255,820 new Ordinary Shares ("
ATM Shares
", together with the Conversion Shares the "
New Shares
") will be issued at an average price of 8.795 pence per ATM Share, in connection with the At-the-Market Facility. As announced by the Company on 5 March 2026 all proceeds from the
At-the-Market Facility will go towards repayment of the Funding Facility.
All Ordinary Shares previously issued pursuant to the At-the-Market Facility have been placed and Axis currently holds no Ordinary Shares.
Chris Chadwick, Chief Executive Officer of Atlas Metals, commented:
"We are grateful to the continued support from Yorkville and the confidence they have demonstrated in Atlas Metals and our strategy through their conversion. The Company remains focused on completing the transformative acquisition of Universal Pozzolanic Silica Alumina Ltd and I look forward to providing further updates in due course as matters progress."
Admission and total voting rights
Application has been made to London Stock Exchange plc ("
London Stock Exchange
") for the admission of 3,368,394 New Shares to be admitted to trading on the main market for listed securities of London Stock Exchange ("
Admission
").
Admission is expected to occur on or around 27 May 2026.
When issued, the New Shares will be fully paid and will rank
pari passu
in all respects with each other and with the existing Ordinary Shares of the Company.
Following Admission, the Company's issued ordinary share capital will comprise 42,549,821 Ordinary Shares, each carrying one vote. The Company does not hold any Ordinary Shares in treasury. The total number of voting rights in the Company following Admission will therefore be 42,549,821. With effect from Admission, this figure may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they determine whether they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.

company_30s.what_they_do: AMG Ltd is a junior uranium exploration and development company. It focuses on acquiring, exploring and developing uranium deposits, primarily in Kyrgyzstan, with plans to bring the assets into production once sufficient funding and regulatory approval are in place.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Uranium is the fuel that powers most of the world's nuclear reactors, and as governments push for low‑carbon energy, demand for uranium is set to rise. AMG’s holdings sit in a region with high-grade resources and a supportive regulatory framework, giving it a clear niche in a market that is both essential and relatively low in competition compared to larger miners.
company_30s.current_state: The company is still in the pre‑production phase, relying on a mix of equity issuances and debt facilities to finance exploration. Recent filings show a focus on securing a funding facility and updating the prospectus, suggesting a push to attract institutional capital. The share price is trading far below the last all‑time high, reflecting the market’s perception of the company’s high risk and long development timeline.

rns_narrative: The company’s recent announcements revolve around two main threads: (1) a series of corporate governance and financing updates—including an updated prospectus, a new funding facility of up to £2.5 million, and changes to the auditor—aimed at improving capital structure and transparency; (2) asset‑specific disclosures such as the latest Kyrgyz uranium asset update and a note on a potential acquisition. Together, these signals indicate that AMG is actively working to shore up its balance sheet and keep the project moving forward, even though it has not yet produced any revenue.

A key theme is the impending prospectus release, which is expected to detail the company’s strategy for moving from exploration to production, including potential partnership or joint‑venture structures.
Another theme is the emphasis on securing liquidity: the funding facility and equity issues point to a need for cash to sustain the project through the next 12–18 months.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet anticipation – traders are watching the prospectus release but largely silent until a concrete funding announcement.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of posts praise the recent funding facility and the potential for a future partnership, suggesting that a successful capital raise could unlock the project’s value.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Several voices warn about dilution from equity issuances, the long timeline to production, and the lack of a clear revenue stream, arguing that the company remains a high‑risk play.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Initial chatter was neutral, but the tone has shifted toward cautious optimism as the prospectus deadline approaches, with traders hoping for a funding win while still wary of the dilution risk.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends shows no recent search interest, meaning the market is largely unaware of AMG’s current developments – a perfect backdrop for a surprise catalyst.

near_term_catalysts: Release of the prospectus, which will outline the capital‑raising plan and any partnership agreements. A positive, detailed prospectus could trigger a buying spree.
Announcement of a successful funding round or a secured loan facility that provides the cash needed to advance drilling and permitting.
A significant increase in trading volume, especially if the shares move above key technical support levels (the 200‑day moving average or a recent swing low).
A favorable shift in uranium prices – a 10–15% jump could make the project more attractive to investors and partners.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume spike of at least 200% above the 20‑day average, a clear breakout from the current support zone, or a press release confirming a new partnership or funding agreement.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a deep‑crash cycle: the price has fallen sharply, trading in a low liquidity zone, and the market has largely forgotten the company. The APEX score suggests a tight price range, indicating that a breakout is possible once a catalyst materializes.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If AMG can secure the funding facility and deliver a detailed, investor‑friendly prospectus, the price could rebound from the current low to roughly 50% of its previous high, yielding a 100% upside. The deep crash creates a steep mean‑reversion opportunity, and the company’s unique uranium assets give it a clear long‑term value driver.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The main risks are dilution from equity issuances, a prolonged development timeline, and the possibility that the funding facility is insufficient to reach the next milestone. If the company fails to raise capital, the price could slide further toward its all‑time low, and the risk of a complete write‑down remains.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss: 30% of the current price if the funding fails or the project stalls. Potential gain: 100% if the price recovers to the halfway mark between the current level and the last high. Risk‑reward ratio roughly 1:3, which is highly favorable for a contrarian play.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the share price closes above the 200‑day moving average and volume exceeds the 20‑day average by at least 50%. Size the position so that a 30% drop would wipe out the trade, and set a stop 15% below the entry to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until the prospectus is released or a funding announcement is made. If the company announces a partnership or a significant funding round, consider a small entry with a tight stop at the recent swing low.
playbook.if_passing: If there is a clear indication that the company is being delisted or that the project is abandoned (e.g., a court filing or a formal liquidation notice), exit immediately.

ai_insights.bottom_line: AMG is a deep‑crash uranium play that could deliver a 100% upside once a funding win or prospectus release sparks a mean‑reversion rally, but only if the company overcomes dilution and a long development timeline.
